message:
fix(fcp-store): drop empty zone_index entries on object delete (br-A9 sweep)

MemoryObjectStore::delete retained (zone_id, Vec) entries in
zone_index even after retain drained the Vec. Effect: the map grew
monotonically with cardinality of zones EVER seen on a host, not
currently-active zones. Bounded but permanent leak on long-running
hosts that cycle zones in and out.

Severity: low — not attacker-controlled (ZoneId only flows through
authenticated zone enrollment). Filed as 1-line ship rather than
bead.

Fix: after retain empties the Vec, remove(zone_id) drops the entry
while still inside the same write-lock guard. Single regression
test (delete_last_object_in_zone_drops_zone_index_entry) asserts
the empty entry is gone AND re-inserting an object for the same
zone still works.

Discovered during fresh-eyes review of crates/fcp-store/src/ for
persistence integrity, GC correctness, capability checks, and
repair partial-failure handling. Other surfaces clean:

- durable.rs WAL/snapshot uses sync_all -> atomic-rename ->
  sync_parent_dir; checksum + version + monotonic-seq guards on
  replay; bounded-line read prevents OOM on torn WAL
- gc.rs collect_evicted_zone deletes object before symbols
  (concurrent readers see NotFound, never live-object-no-symbols);
  restore_object_snapshot tolerates AlreadyExists
- repair.rs next_repair holds queue write lock through
  rate-limit/power-state checks (no TOCTOU)
- quarantine.rs single-RwLock zone-keyed map; promote -> remove
  atomic; promotion-reason validation is metadata-shape (caller
  responsible for cryptographic auth)
- symbol_store.rs lock ordering (objects -> obj -> used_bytes)
  consistent across put_symbol / delete_symbol / delete_object /
  scrub paths

915 fcp-store tests passing.
